name: Sonatype Nexus Repository (Community/OSS)
license: EPL-1.0 (open-source core via sonatype/nexus-public); Community Edition binary distributed under Sonatype Community Edition EULA
cost: Community Edition is free (no credit card). Pro starts ~$1,620/year plus consumption-based charges.
language: Java (JVM; Apache Karaf/OSGi, Jetty; runs on Java 21 — bundled since v3.87, required for v3.93.0; Java 17 support ended at v3.86.x)
perf_notes: JVM application (not a lightweight binary). Community Edition enforces hard usage caps: ~100,000 components / 200,000 requests/day in v3.77+, lowered to ~40,000 components / 100,000 requests/day in v3.87+; once exceeded you cannot add new components until usage drops below the cap (failed requests still count). Sonatype notes file-system blob stores can be up to ~3x faster than object-store blob stores even in-region; in Community the only object store is S3 (Azure and Google Cloud Storage blob stores are Pro-only).
scale_notes: Multi-node / HA is possible in Community by backing the instance with external PostgreSQL and a shared blob store (e.g., S3); the Community download page lists "Resiliency and High Availability Deployment" and "Zero Downtime Upgrades." However, guaranteed HA, disaster recovery, and blob-store replication are positioned as Pro features, and Community is subject to the component/request caps above. Default small deployments use an embedded H2 database. Nexus is stateful, so scaling is coordinated multi-node rather than free stateless horizontal scaling.
summary: Sonatype's free, self-hosted Java artifact repository manager that hosts, proxies, and groups PyPI (and dozens of other) package formats from one distribution, built on an open-source core with usage caps that steer larger teams toward the paid Pro tier.
extras: Group (virtual) repositories merge proxy + hosted + other groups behind one PyPI URL with configurable resolution order
Routing rules let you allow/block path patterns on proxy repos, mitigating dependency-confusion within Nexus alone

Multi-format manager: Maven, npm, Docker/OCI, NuGet, Helm, APT/yum, raw, plus newer Cargo (Rust), Hugging Face, Composer (PHP), Conan v2, conda, and (2026) Go, Ansible, Swift, Terraform, and Alpine/APK
Cleanup policies + scheduled tasks (soft-delete then daily compact-blob-store; deleted blobs purged after ~3 days, underlying object retention up to ~45 days)
Hosted-repo deployment policy (allow-redeploy / disable-redeploy / read-only) provides overwrite protection
Built-in Prometheus endpoint (/service/metrics/prometheus), JSON metrics, status/writable health endpoints, and system-information API
Official sonatype/nexus3 Docker image and community/Sonatype Helm charts

PyPI proxy/hosted/group repos serve PEP 658 inline metadata and the PEP 691 JSON Simple API (with PEP 714 field names) from v3.93+, while staying backward compatible with PEP 503 HTML clients; release notes cite pip 23.1+, uv 0.1.0+, and Poetry 1.8+ compatibility. v3.93 also fixed non-normalized-name index refresh and chained-proxy retrieval. PEP 700 JSON extras (size/upload-time) and PEP 740 attestations are not documented
Vulnerability/malware scanning, quarantine, SBOM, and license policy are NOT in Nexus Repository itself; they require the separately-licensed Sonatype Repository Firewall / Lifecycle (IQ Server)

Pro-only (not in Community): SAML SSO and OAuth2/OIDC login, User Tokens (token auth for pip/twine), Azure & Google Cloud Storage blob stores, and guaranteed HA/DR/blob-replication. Community DOES include LDAP/AD authentication, RBAC roles/content-selectors, file + S3 blob stores, and audit logging (audit.log file + Audit Log REST API); it authenticates pip/twine via username/password (HTTP Basic)
